seoTitle: How to Remove Unwanted Bank Accounts From QuickBooks
metaDescription: Disconnect a live bank feed and make an old account inactive in QuickBooks Online without losing your historical transaction data.
intro: Closing out an old checking account or credit card in QuickBooks Online involves two separate steps: disconnecting the live feed, then making the account inactive in your Chart of Accounts. This guide walks through both, so your dashboard stays clean while your historical records remain fully intact. It's for business owners tidying up their books after discontinuing a card or account.

steps.heading: Open the Bank Transactions dashboard
steps.body: Go to Transactions, then Bank Transactions, to see your connected live bank feeds.

steps.heading: Disconnect the live feed
steps.body: Click the pencil edit icon on the unwanted account card, select Edit Account Info, check Disconnect this account on save, and save.

steps.heading: Navigate to Chart of Accounts
steps.body: Go back to Transactions and open Chart of Accounts to find the account's ledger entry.

steps.heading: Make the account inactive
steps.body: Find the account row, click the dropdown arrow in the Action column, and select Make Inactive to confirm the closure.

steps.heading: Verify it's hidden from your list
steps.body: Click the filter gear icon above the accounts list and uncheck Include Inactive to confirm the account no longer clutters your view.

faq.question: Will making an account inactive delete my historical data?
faq.answer: No, all past transactions and reports tied to the account are preserved even after it's marked inactive.

faq.question: Do I need to disconnect the feed before making the account inactive?
faq.answer: Yes, disconnecting the live sync first prevents QuickBooks from continuing to pull in new transactions for an account you're closing.

faq.question: Can I reactivate the account later if I need to?
faq.answer: Yes, inactive accounts can be reactivated at any time from the same filter menu in Chart of Accounts.

faq.question: What happens to a leftover balance on the account?
faq.answer: You may need to zero out or transfer any residual balance before the deactivation is fully clean, especially if the account still shows an open amount.
